methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
ABL Bio is a public-stage biotech company (KOSDAQ-listed) founded by Sang Hoon Lee, having raised over $5 billion through IPO and licensing agreements. The Biotech & Health sector comprises 11 comparables—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Abridge, Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, and Freenome.

Standard valuation triangulation methods proved limited. Peer-Median analysis was inapplicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist, none met the minimum three-datapoint threshold when filtered by stage and valuation tier. Public-Comp Anchoring could not proceed due to missing revenue disclosure; target revenue is not on file, which is typical for private companies. The public sector median P/S multiple stands at 6.1x across the three public tickers, but remains unusable without ABL Bio's corresponding revenue figure.

The absence of tight-peer clustering and undisclosed revenue prevent quantitative range synthesis at this stage. A valuation conclusion would require either: disclosure of ABL Bio's annual or trailing-twelve-month revenue; identification of transaction comps within the private peer set; or access to proprietary financing rounds with known valuations. The key uncertainties are revenue scale and comparable transaction evidence within the frontier-biotech cohort. Further work should prioritize revenue confirmation and private-market transaction data before estimating a supported valuation band.
